ip_outcome_note: Card name directly names the depicted character as Bardock. Bandai official listing confirms Saiyan/Great Ape traits and Bardock Saga era stamp. Frontmatter updated: ip_verified: true.

trivia_bullets.text: P-035 is a Judge Promo card distributed through Official Tournament Pack Vol.3 (Cross Worlds season), available to sanctioned tournament judges from March 9, 2018. Not obtainable through retail; foil variant is same art with foil treatment.
sources: dbs-cardgame.com: Shop Tournaments Vol.3 event page

trivia_bullets.text: Yellow Battle card, power 20,000, energy cost 4, Combo Power 10,000. Traits: Saiyan/Great Ape. Skills: Double Strike + Blocker. Auto: after comboing with this card during your turn, evolve an existing Bardock Battle Card into P-035 — bypassing full energy cost for the Double Strike/Blocker package.

The Great Ape (Oozaru) depicted on P-035 references Bardock's transformation in the 1990 Toei TV special Dragon Ball Z: Bardock — The Father of Goku, where Bardock and his squad transform into Great Apes to destroy a civilization. Bardock is unusually depicted as retaining memory of his Great Ape actions — atypical for the species. Canon status of the special is fan-debated; Kanzenshuu community notes the ending scenes mirror the manga, lending partial canonical weight.

Bardock's characterization was rewritten by Akira Toriyama in the 2013 bonus chapter Dragon Ball Minus (collected in Jaco the Galactic Patrolman), making Bardock a protective father who arranges Kakarot's escape to Earth — inspired by Toriyama's own affection for the TV-special character. The DBS Card Game's 'Bardock Saga' era stamp covers both iterations; P-035's Oozaru-form depiction anchors the original special's pre-revision version.
sources: CBR: 'Dragon Ball Z: What's Still Canon From Bardock: The Father of Goku?'

trivia_bullets.text: P-035 is the only Great-Ape-depicted card in the BBL corpus as of this wave — the sole Oozaru-form anchor in the Saiyan cluster. Its Evolve-via-combo skill mechanically ties it to any Bardock-named Battle Card from BT1-BT3.
